_comment: Tier-1 objective gates derived from JTBD-4 (1-requirements.md) + the compliance deep-dive. Deterministic + offline (no fal): Pillow spec verdicts, c2patool/disclosure outputs, and the FTC fake-review gate. Expectations are checkable against the JSON/markdown artifacts and the known-good/known-bad fixtures under evals/fixtures/.

evals.prompt: Run the Amazon main-image spec check on the known-GOOD packshot (evals/fixtures/good-packshot.jpg — pure white background, product fills ~88% of a 2000px frame, no text). Report the verdict.
evals.expected_output: A JSON verdict with overall_pass=true: bg_pass=true (exact RGB 255,255,255 at all 8 sample points), fill_pass=true (>=0.85), res_ok=true (>=1600px), text_flag=false.
expectations: the verdict JSON has overall_pass=true
bg_pass=true with all 8 sampled pixels equal to [255,255,255]
fill_pass=true (product bbox >= 0.85 of the frame) and res_ok=true (longest side >= 1600)
text_flag=false (a single frame-filling product is NOT mis-flagged as a watermark)

evals.prompt: Run the Amazon main-image spec check on the known-BAD image (evals/fixtures/bad-offwhite.jpg — off-white RGB 250,252,253 background, small product, 800px). Report the verdict and what fails.
evals.expected_output: A JSON verdict with overall_pass=false: bg_pass=false (off-white 250 is NOT exact 255 and fails Amazon), fill_pass=false, res_ok=false. The reasons are stated; off-white is not silently passed.
expectations: the verdict JSON has overall_pass=false
bg_pass=false specifically because the background is off-white (250,252,253), NOT exact (255,255,255) — 254/250 must fail
res_ok=false (800px < 1600) and fill_pass=false are both reported

evals.prompt: Run the spec check on the watermark fixture (evals/fixtures/watermark.jpg — a clean product PLUS a logo bar burned into the top-left corner). Does the text/logo/watermark flag fire?
evals.expected_output: text_flag=true with a reason naming a second ink element (watermark/logo/inset) separate from the product; the flag is advisory (heuristic, not OCR) and routes to human review.
expectations: text_flag=true
the text_note explains a second ink element separate from the product was detected (possible watermark/logo/inset)
the verdict states the flag is a heuristic, not OCR, and never certifies the image is text-free

evals.prompt: Repair the transparent cutout (evals/fixtures/cutout-transparent.png — a product on a transparent background, what Bria RMBG outputs) into an Amazon-compliant main image, then re-check it.
evals.expected_output: The repaired JPEG flattens the cutout onto pure white; on re-check bg_pass=true and metadata is stripped. The repair preserves the product pixels and never re-backgrounds it generatively.
expectations: a repaired JPEG is written and on re-check bg_pass=true (background is now exact 255,255,255)
the repair flattened the transparency onto pure white and stripped metadata (no generative re-background was used)
the product pixels are unchanged (the repair only composites over white)

evals.prompt: Generate the per-channel disclosure text and C2PA stamp for an AI-generated product image targeting channels amazon,meta,tiktok and jurisdictions us,eu,ny.
evals.expected_output: disclosure.md with the verbatim Amazon 'substantially modified' note (flagged advisory / G1881 login-gated), Meta 'AI-generated' label, TikTok AIGC note, and a dated EU (2026-08-02) + NY (2026-06-09) jurisdiction note; a <name>-cc.jpg with a C2PA manifest (or, if c2patool is absent, c2pa_signed=false recorded honestly).
expectations: disclosure.md contains the correct disclosure string for EACH requested channel (Amazon substantially-modified note, Meta AI-generated, TikTok AIGC)
the Amazon gen-AI note is flagged ADVISORY with the G1881-login-gated / not-machine-confirmed caveat
the jurisdiction note is DATED: EU AI Act Art.50 operative 2026-08-02 and NY SB-8420A effective 2026-06-09 are stated with their dates
either a -cc.jpg with a c2patool-readable manifest is produced, OR c2pa_signed=false is recorded with 'vendor c2patool at build' (never silently claimed as signed)

evals.prompt: Run the multi-channel linter with ad copy that contains an AI-generated review ('review generated by our AI assistant' attributed to a named 'verified buyer'). What is the FTC verdict and the overall verdict?
evals.expected_output: The FTC 16 CFR Part 465 gate returns BLOCK (an AI-generated testimonial by a reviewer presented as a real customer is a hard block); the overall verdict is BLOCK; the hit cites 465.2.
expectations: the ftc verdict is BLOCK (an AI-generated review/testimonial is never PASSed)
at least one hit cites rule 465.2 and explains the AI-generated / non-existent-reviewer violation
the overall_verdict is BLOCK and never_auto_publish=true (the verdict is never an instruction to publish)

evals.prompt: Run the full pre-flight (spec check -> disclosure stamp -> multi-channel lint) on the good packshot for channels amazon,etsy,meta,tiktok,shopify and jurisdictions us,eu,ca,ny with NO ad copy supplied. Report the per-channel verdict.
evals.expected_output: preflight.json with a PASS/FIX/FLAG row per channel keyed off the spec + C2PA result, ftc=no_copy_supplied (not a PASS), a dated 4-jurisdiction note, advisory rows flagged confirmed=false, and never_auto_publish=true.
expectations: preflight.json has one verdict row for each of the 5 requested channels
ftc is reported as no_copy_supplied (NOT a PASS) when no copy is given
interpretive/advisory rows (Amazon gen-AI threshold, Etsy, channel sub-policies) carry confirmed=false
the jurisdiction_note covers all 4 requested jurisdictions with dates and never asserts a not-yet-operative rule (EU/CA 2026-08-02) as binding before its date
never_auto_publish=true and the output is a report, never an upload
